setting.note: In a crumbling house in Luanda, Félix Ventura makes his living selling invented pasts and distinguished genealogies to Angola's new bourgeoisie, watched over by the narrator, a gecko on the wall who was once a man. When a stranger buys a whole new identity, the fabricated past collides with the real, violent history of the civil war. Agualusa sets his sly, dreamlike novel in the real Luanda: the decaying villa, the city of the newly powerful, and the buried wartime crimes that surface through the lies.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. José Eduardo Agualusa, first published 2004 (O Vendedor de Passados). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Book of Chameleons, José Eduardo Agualusa (2004). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The shifting green of the chameleon and the wall-gecko who tells the tale, the colour of a creature that becomes whatever it needs to be, like the pasts Félix sells. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
